card.intuition: The step-by-step process the browser takes to convert raw HTML, CSS, and JS into actual pixels on your screen.
card.analogy: An artist's workflow: first they sketch the outline (DOM), then they decide the colors (CSSOM), then they draw the grid (Layout), and finally they fill in the paint (Painting).
when_to_use: Understanding the impact of JavaScript on loading performance
gotchas: JavaScript can block the pipeline at the 'DOM' stage if it's placed in the <head> without defer/async
Changing layout-affecting properties (like width/height) forces the browser to go back several steps in the pipeline, which is expensive

exercise.prompt: Identify the five main steps of the render pipeline in order.
exercise.test_code:
assert "Layout" in pipeline[2]
assert "Paint" in pipeline[3]
print("PASS")
